title: Security in Express.js Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the essential security practices for Node.js and Express.js applications.

questions.question: Which popular middleware sets various HTTP headers for enhanced security?
options: Cors
Helmet
Body-Parser
Bcrypt

questions.question: What's the best way to store a user's password in a database?
options: In plain text for easy recovery.
Encrypted with a reversible algorithm.
Hashed with a strong algorithm like Bcrypt.
As a JSON string.

questions.question: What does 'httpOnly: true' do for a cookie?
options: It only allows the cookie to be sent over HTTPS.
It prevents local JavaScript from accessing the cookie, helping to prevent XSS attacks.
It makes the cookie expire after a certain amount of time.
It ensures the cookie can only be sent from the same origin.

questions.question: What is the purpose of rate limiting in an Express application?
options: To make the application faster.
To prevent Brute-Force and Denial of Service (DoS) attacks.
To automatically generate documentation for the API.
To limit the number of users on the site.

questions.question: How can you hide that your application is running on Express to attackers?
options: By using a custom domain.
By calling app.disable('x-powered-by').
By writing all code in a single file.
It's not possible to hide it.
